mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3; reported active in FLT3-ITD AML including MOLM-13
additional_notes: Use log10 serial dilutions; example points given in nM (0.5–10,000 nM)

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, PDGFR, VEGFR family kinases
Inhibits SRC and ABL family kinases; reported activity in some AML models
Inhibits BCR-ABL with additional activity against FLT3 and KIT-driven AML cells
Inhibits BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase; weaker activity in AML but used as reference compound
Clinically used FLT3 inhibitor relevant to FLT3-ITD AML including MOLM-13
Highly potent and selective FLT3 inhibitor; strong activity in FLT3-ITD AML cell lines

mechanism: Clinically approved FLT3 inhibitor; active in MOLM-13 and other FLT3-ITD lines
Potent inhibitor of BCR-ABL, FLT3, FGFR and others with strong in vitro AML activity
Selectively inhibits anti-apoptotic protein BCL-2, promoting apoptosis in AML cells

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 signaling, impairing cytokine-dependent AML/myeloproliferative proliferation
Inhibits MEK1 and MEK2 in the MAPK signaling pathway, often activated in AML
Inhibits MEK1/2, blocking MAPK pathway signaling in leukemia and other hematologic models
Inhibits mTOR, targeting the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway frequently active in AML
Inhibits mTOR signaling, exerting anti-proliferative effects in AML cells
Inhibits the 26S proteasome, inducing apoptosis in AML cell lines at low-nanomolar concentrations

mechanism: Second-generation irreversible proteasome inhibitor, cytotoxic against AML blasts and cell lines
additional_notes: Core suggested range 0.5–500 nM; extend to 1–2 µM if needed

Immunomodulatory and anti-leukemic effects, including differentiation in subsets of AML/MDS
